sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Real Madrid, currently sitting at 2nd in the league with 75 points, host Mallorca, who are positioned 9th with 47 points, at the iconic Santiago Bernabéu in Madrid. This matchup promises to be an intriguing clash given the disparity in league standings. The referee, Javier Alberola Rojas, is known for averaging 3.6 yellow cards and 0.17 red cards per game, which could influence the flow of the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last 5 encounters, Real Madrid have dominated with 3 wins, while Mallorca managed just 1 victory, and 1 game ended in a draw. Their most recent meeting on January 9, 2025, saw Real Madrid triumph 3-0 at home, with Jude Bellingham earning the Player of the Match title with an impressive 8.7 Sofascore rating. Historically, Real Madrid have been unbeaten in the last 4 head-to-heads, often scoring first in 4 of the last 5 matches against Mallorca.

sections.subtitle: Real Madrid’s Key Players
sections.text: Real Madrid boast a formidable lineup led by Kylian Mbappé, who tops the league with 27 goals and ranks 3rd with a 7.65 average rating. Jude Bellingham, another standout with a league rank of 4 for assists (8) and 5 for fouls drawn per game (2.1), has been exceptional against Mallorca, averaging a 7.825 Sofascore rating in the last 5 matchups. Federico Valverde and Luka Modrić also contribute significantly, with Modrić ranking 4th in the league for big chances created (15). However, the absence of key players like Vinícius Júnior, who ranks 2nd for successful dribbles (2.8 per game), and Rodrygo could impact their attacking fluidity.

sections.subtitle: Mallorca’s Key Contributors
sections.text: Mallorca will rely on forward Vedat Muriqi, who has netted 7 goals this season, alongside Cyle Larin with 6 goals, to challenge Real Madrid’s defense. Midfielder Sergi Darder, with a recent form rating of 7.54 over the last 5 matches and 5 assists this season, will be crucial in creating opportunities. Dani Rodríguez also stands out with 7 assists, though Mallorca’s overall attacking stats pale compared to Real Madrid. Their defense, anchored by Antonio Raíllo with a solid 7.12 average rating, will need to be at its best to contain Real Madrid’s firepower.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Statistics
sections.text: Real Madrid have scored 72 goals this season, ranking 2nd in the league, and dominate possession with an average of 59.97%, also ranking 2nd. They excel in creating big chances (122, rank 2) and converting 37.7% of them, though they’ve missed 76 big chances, ranking 19th for inefficiency. Mallorca, on the other hand, have struggled to keep clean sheets in their last 4 games against Real Madrid and in 3 consecutive general matches. Their goal distribution shows strength late in games, with 27.5% of goals scored between the 76th and 90th minutes, which could be a factor if the game remains close.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Real Madrid’s dominance in ground duels (55.84% won, rank 1) and aerial duels (56.38% won, rank 1), coupled with their league-leading accurate passes percentage of 89.73%, suggests they will control the game’s tempo. Their average of 5.69 corners per game (rank 2) indicates they’ll likely pressure Mallorca’s defense through set pieces. Mallorca’s recent trend of games with less than 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 away matches hints at a defensive approach, possibly aiming to frustrate Real Madrid with compact play. However, Real Madrid’s fast breaks (1.29 per game, rank 3) could exploit any gaps.

sections.subtitle: In-Form Players to Watch
sections.text: For Real Madrid, Kylian Mbappé is in scintillating form, averaging a 7.44 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches with 6 goals. Youngster Arda Güler has also impressed, averaging 7.38 with 2 goals and 2 assists in the same period. On Mallorca’s side, Sergi Darder’s recent form (7.54 rating, 2 goals, 1 assist) makes him a creative threat, while goalkeeper Leo Román’s exceptional 9.4 average rating over the last 5 matches could be crucial in keeping the scoreline tight.

sections.subtitle: Match Expectations
sections.text: Given Real Madrid’s superior league position, home advantage, and historical edge over Mallorca, they are the clear favorites to win. Their ability to score first in 4 of the last 5 head-to-heads and overall matches adds to their likelihood of taking an early lead. Mallorca’s defensive resilience might keep the game close initially, but their lack of clean sheets against Real Madrid (4 consecutive games) and overall form suggest they’ll struggle to contain the hosts. Expect a match with moderate disciplinary action, as head-to-heads have seen more than 4.5 cards in 5 of the last 7 encounters.
